title: Celtics get good news on Jaylen Brown’s Achilles tendinitis injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown cleared to play against Atlanta Hawks after missing two games due to Achilles tendinitis.
Derrick White listed as probable for Celtics game versus Hawks with a right knee contusion.
Jayson Tatum out for rest due to right Achilles repair management, not playing back-to-back games.

title: Major pharmaceutical company to cut nearly 250 Massachusetts jobs
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Takeda Pharmaceuticals plans to lay off nearly 250 employees in Massachusetts as part of a larger $1.25 billion restructuring plan.
The layoffs at Takeda's 500 Kendall St. location in Cambridge are set to begin in July and may continue into 2027.
Other life sciences firms in Massachusetts, including Moderna and Thermo Fisher Scientific, have also recently announced job cuts.

title: Lexington to cut a slew of teachers as town prepares to build $660M high school
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lexington Public Schools plans to eliminate 65 full-time positions next fiscal year due to budget constraints.
160 teachers without professional status received non-renewal notices amid warnings of a prolonged staffing crisis.
Recent tax increase for a $660 million high school limits options for closing the district's budget gap.

title: At 22, she’s running Boston for her mom — and a future without Alzheimer’s
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Ella Wright, 22, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her mother and raise awareness for early-onset Alzheimer's.
Wright's mother was diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer's in her forties, impacting the family deeply.
She aims to advocate for caregivers, increase research funding, and support other families affected by Alzheimer's.

title: Plymouth officer charged with child rape suspended by POST Commission
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Plymouth officer Samantha Pelrine suspended by POST Commission after arrest on child rape charges.
Pelrine and Daniel Forand accused of grooming and sexually abusing a minor in their custody from 2018 to 2025.
The couple, out on bail, has been ordered to avoid contact with the alleged victim and surrender firearms.

title: After 7 e-bike incidents in 10 days, Mass. police chief warns of impending ‘tragedy’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Milford experienced seven e-bike incidents in ten days, prompting police warnings about potential tragedies.
A 15-year-old lost control of an e-bike, colliding with a car on Highlight Street with no injuries reported.
Massachusetts law treats e-bikes like bicycles; riders must be 16 and wear helmets, but safety concerns grow.

title: Bridgewater police warn of Tom Brady impersonation scam
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Bridgewater police warn of a scam involving a Tom Brady impersonator after a local family was defrauded.
The victim sent gift cards to the scammer posing as the former Patriots quarterback, according to police.
Police advised the family on preventing further losses; FTC warns against sending money to unknown contacts.

title: This Boston Marathon runner is supporting children in their hardest times
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Maria Castaneda, 52, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for The Children's Room.
Castaneda, originally from Mexico, feels a strong connection to The Children's Room's mission.
The Children's Room provides support to children and families coping with grief and difficult times.

title: Review and setlist: Lady Gaga’s Mayhem Ball brought the spectacle, but Boston brought the heart
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lady Gaga brought her Mayhem Ball tour to TD Garden, performing a nearly three-hour show mixing hits and new material.
Fans organized a surprise tribute for Gaga's 40th birthday, holding signs that moved her to tears during the concert.
Gaga's setlist included 'Bloody Mary,' 'Shallow,' and 'Bad Romance,' closing with 'Sour Candy' as a Boston-specific encore.

title: Would you try the new couch-style seating on United? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines will introduce 'Relax Row' couch-style seating in economy class in 2027.
A Boston.com poll showed 61% of readers are eager to try the new seating option.
Concerns include potential noise from families, safety questions, and high costs for booking the entire row.
